compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
# Cut the v0.8.0 release (minor bump)

Cut the next release of `nava-rebar`, incrementing the **minor** version `0.7.1 -> 0.8.0`,
using the new dispatch-from-`main` release-authorization workflow (story fd84). Bump every
version manifest + enforced doc marker in lockstep, promote the curated CHANGELOG
`[Unreleased]` section to `[0.8.0]`, correct the release runbook to the Gerrit landing flow,
land the change on `main` through the Gerrit two-vote gate, then dispatch `release.yml` from
`main` with `version=0.8.0` and drive it to a completed publish.

## Why

`main` is at `0.7.1`; the accumulated user-facing changes in `CHANGELOG.md` `[Unreleased]`
warrant a release. Pre-1.0 SemVer permits breaking changes in a minor bump, so `0.8.0` is
correct. The new `release.yml` only publishes from a `workflow_dispatch`-on-`main` run whose
`authorize` job asserts version-lockstep, so the manifests must equal the dispatch `version`
input on `main` before the run. Separately, `docs/releasing.md` step 1 still documents the
**old** `git push origin main` flow, which contradicts this repo's Gerrit-only landing rule
(direct pushes to `main` are rejected) â€” the runbook must be corrected as part of cutting a
release correctly.

## What / Scope

- `pyproject.toml` â€” `[project].version` `0.7.1` -> `0.8.0`.
- `server.json` â€” top-level `version` and `packages[0].version` -> `0.8.0`.
- `docs/api-stability.md` â€” the prose marker `currently 0.7.1` -> `currently 0.8.0`. This is
  **enforced**: `tests/unit/test_version_sync.py` asserts the doc marker equals
  `pyproject.toml`'s version, so a missed bump fails `make test` and blocks the Gerrit
  `Verified` vote.
- `CHANGELOG.md` â€” retitle `## [Unreleased]` to `## [0.8.0] - <landing date>` and open a fresh
  empty `## [Unreleased]` above it; update the footer reference links (add
  `[0.8.0]: .../compare/v0.7.1...v0.8.0`, retarget `[unreleased]` to `.../compare/v0.8.0...HEAD`).
  The section is already hand-curated so no `git-cliff` regeneration is needed. The date is
  set from the actual commit date at commit time (`git log -1 --format=%as`), not hardcoded.
- `docs/releasing.md` â€” correct the release runbook to the Gerrit flow: step 1 must **not**
  say `git push origin main`; instead bump + commit with the `rebar-ticket:`/DCO trailers and
  land through Gerrit (`git push gerrit HEAD:refs/for/main`, earn `LLM-Review +1` AND
  `Verified +1`, then Submit), consistent with the "Release-source authorization" section and
  CONTRIBUTING.md. Fix any other step that implies a direct push to `main`.
- Land the one commit on `main` via Gerrit, then dispatch `release.yml --ref main -f
  version=0.8.0`, approve the `pypi` required-reviewer gate, and monitor to completion. No
  workflow/product-code logic changes â€” this is a release cut plus a runbook correction.

## Failure recovery (release is partly irreversible)

PyPI is immutable: a published `0.8.0` can only be **yanked**, never replaced â€” a bad release
is fixed by yanking + cutting `0.8.1`. If a run publishes to PyPI but a later job
(`github_release`/`mcp_registry`) fails, do **not** re-dispatch the whole workflow (it would
re-attempt the already-done PyPI publish); re-run only the failed job, or complete it manually
(`gh release create v0.8.0 â€¦` / re-run the mcp publish step), since PyPI/MCP versions are
idempotent-by-existence.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `pyproject.toml` `[project].version == "0.8.0"` (verify:
  `python -c "import tomllib;assert tomllib.load(open('pyproject.toml','rb'))['project']['version']=='0.8.0'"`).
- [ ] `server.json` top-level `version` and every `packages[].version` == `"0.8.0"` (verify:
  `python -c "import json;d=json.load(open('server.json'));assert d['version']=='0.8.0' and all(p['version']=='0.8.0' for p in d['packages'])"`).
- [ ] `docs/api-stability.md` prose marker reads `currently 0.8.0` (verify:
  `grep -q 'currently 0.8.0' docs/api-stability.md`).
- [ ] `CHANGELOG.md` has a `## [0.8.0]` section AND a fresh empty `## [Unreleased]` above it
  AND a `[0.8.0]:` footer link (verify:
  `awk '/^## \[Unreleased\]/{u=NR}/^## \[0\.8\.0\]/{v=NR}END{exit !(u>0 && v>u)}' CHANGELOG.md && grep -q '^\[0.8.0\]:' CHANGELOG.md`).
- [ ] `docs/releasing.md` no longer instructs a direct push to `main` for a release and
  documents the Gerrit landing flow (verify:
  `! grep -q 'git push origin main' docs/releasing.md && grep -q 'refs/for/main' docs/releasing.md`).
- [ ] `make lint && make typecheck && make test` pass locally (incl. `test_version_sync`), and
  `python -m build && python -m twine check dist/*` succeed on the bumped tree.
- [ ] [operator-attested] The commit lands on `main` through the Gerrit two-vote gate
  (`LLM-Review +1` AND `Verified +1`) â€” attested by recording the Gerrit change URL + both
  vote outcomes/timestamps as a comment on this ticket.
- [ ] [operator-attested] `release.yml` is dispatched from `main` with `version=0.8.0`; the
  `authorize` job passes all four guards and the run completes with `publish`,
  `github_release`, and `mcp_registry` all successful â€” attested by the workflow run URL + job
  conclusions recorded as a comment on this ticket.
- [ ] PyPI serves `nava-rebar 0.8.0` (verify:
  `test "$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' https://pypi.org/pypi/nava-rebar/0.8.0/json)" = 200`).
- [ ] [operator-attested] A GitHub Release `v0.8.0` (Latest, sdist+wheel attached) + tag exist
  (`gh release view v0.8.0 --repo navapbc/rebar`) and the MCP registry lists `0.8.0` â€” the
  outcomes recorded as a comment on this ticket.

file_impact.reason: correct release runbook to the Gerrit landing flow

compiled_state.title: Cut the v0.8.0 release (minor bump)
